task_completion.visual_evidence: In the NextGen Connect Administrator interface, a channel with the exact name 'ADT_CSV_Export' is visible in the Channels list or the channel edit screen.

task_completion.description: Configure the source as a TCP Listener on port 6661.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Source tab of the channel configuration, the Connector Type is set to 'TCP Listener' and the Port field is set to '6661'.

task_completion.description: Configure the destination as a File Writer to the specified CSV file in append mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Destinations tab, the Connector Type is 'File Writer', the Directory/File Name fields point to '/home/ga/output/patient_demographics.csv', and the 'Append to file' (or similar) option is selected/checked.

task_completion.description: Extract demographics from the PID segment and format as CSV with the required header.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Transformer, Message Builder, or Scripts view, there is logic (JavaScript or mapping steps) extracting fields from the HL7 PID segment (e.g., PID.3, PID.5) and constructing a CSV string with the header 'PatientID,LastName,FirstName,DOB,Gender,Street,City,State,Zip,Phone,SSN'.
The NextGen Connect Dashboard shows the 'ADT_CSV_Export' channel in the list with a status indicating it is deployed or started.

integrity.description: The agent used the NextGen Connect Administrator interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the NextGen Connect Administrator GUI (typically a Java application launched from the browser), rather than a custom terminal script bypassing the software.

integrity.description: The agent did not hardcode the expected test outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The transformation scripts or mappings use variables or HL7 message references (like msg['PID']) rather than hardcoded strings like 'MRN10045' or 'JOHNSON'.

integrity.description: The configuration is genuinely entered into the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The settings are visible in the actual input fields and tabs of the NextGen Connect GUI, not just written in a text editor or notes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires configuring a NextGen Connect (formerly Mirth Connect) channel. The channel name must be 'ADT_CSV_Export'. The source must be a TCP Listener on port 6661. The destination must be a File Writer saving to '/home/ga/output/patient_demographics.csv' in append mode. The transformation step must extract patient demographics from the HL7 PID segment and output a CSV with the exact header: 'PatientID,LastName,FirstName,DOB,Gender,Street,City,State,Zip,Phone,SSN'.
